info.title: Netlas API
info.description:
## Environment To interact with the Netlas API, you will need an API client or at least the `curl` command-line utility. Alternatively, you can use the **Netlas CLI tool** or the **Netlas Python SDK**. This specification includes code samples for all of these options. ### Setting Up an API Client Most modern API clients support **OpenAPI v3.1** imports. Click **Download** at the top of this page, then import the file into your preferred API client. ✅ We've confirmed that imports work smoothly in the following clients: | API Client | Steps to Import | |-------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | **Postman** | In *Import Settings*, set *Folder Organization* to *Tags* for better structure. | | **Insomnia** | *Collections* → *Create*, then click the collection name in the top-left corner and choose *Import From File*. | | **Bruno** | Click *Import Collection* → *OpenAPI V3 File*, then choose a local folder to store the workspace. | | **Scalar** | Don’t forget to configure the API key in the collection's authentication settings. | ⚠️ For tools that still expect **OpenAPI 3.0**, use the OpenAPI 3.0 JSON, for example with Thunder Client, RapidAPI for Mac (Paw), and other older OpenAPI-based import and code generation workflows. ### Netlas CLI Tool The Netlas CLI tool is ideal for users who prefer the command line or Bash scripting. It allows you to interact with Netlas just like any other terminal application. To install the CLI via Homebrew: ```bash brew tap netlas-io/netlas brew install netlas ``` Or install it using pipx: ```bash pipx install netlas ``` ### Netlas Python SDK The Netlas Python SDK is designed for Python developers. It provides a convenient way to work with the API, with built-in handling for common errors. Install the SDK using pip: ```bash pip install netlas ``` > The Netlas CLI tool is included with the SDK, so it becomes available automatically after SDK installation. Both the Netlas Python SDK and CLI tool are open-source and available under the MIT license in the Github Netlas repository. ## Authentication Each registered Netlas user receives a unique API key. You can find your API key on the profile page, accessible from the top-right menu in the web application. ![API-key access](/assets/images/api_key-d.png) Include your API key as a **Bearer token** in the `Authorization` header to authorize your requests. The `curl` examples in this manual will work as-is if you export the API key as the `NETLAS_API_KEY` environment variable: ```bash export NETLAS_API_KEY=put_your_key_here curl -X 'GET' "https://app.netlas.io/api/host/" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $NETLAS_API_KEY" ``` Netlas API authorization follows RFC 6750, _The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework: Bearer Token Usage_. > The `X-API-Key` header was previously used for authentication and is still supported, but **considered deprecated** and may be removed in future versions. If you are using the Netlas CLI or SDK, securely save your API key locally: ```bash netlas savekey "YOUR_API_KEY" ``` The Netlas CLI tool automatically uses the saved key. To fetch saved key in Python: ```python import netlas api_key = netlas.helpers.get_api_key() ``` ⚠️ **FYI:** You may be eligible for a special subscription if you're using Netlas for educational purposes, building an integration, or writing for a blog or journal.
Read more → ## Rate Limits To ensure fair usage and optimal performance, the Netlas API enforces the following rate limits: - **60 requests per minute** for search and count operations, excluding certificate searches. - **3 requests per minute** for search and count operations involving [certificate data collections](#tag/Certificates). If these limits are exceeded, the API responds with __Error 429 (Too Many Requests)__ and includes a `Retry-After` header indicating the number of seconds to wait before retrying. The Netlas CLI and SDK are configured by default to automatically handle rate limit errors. When using the SDK, you can customize this behavior by setting the `throttling` and `retry` parameters in the search methods. This allows you to either disable automatic handling or specify the number of retry attempts. To manage rate limiting manually you you can catch and handle the `ThrottlingError` exception in your code: ```python import netlas import time nc = netlas.Netlas() for i in range(65): try: nc.host(host=None, fields=["*"], exclude_fields=True, throttling=False) except netlas.ThrottlingError as e: time.sleep(e.retry_after) # Wait for the specified time before retrying ``` ⚠️ **Important:** Always handle Netlas API errors correctly. Ignoring rate limits or other errors may result in temporary access restrictions. Read more about blocking policy in the FAQ section.

get.description: Retrieve the latest available data for `{host}`.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The IP address or domain name for which data should be retrieved.

x-codeSamples.source:
# IP query example
curl -s -X GET \
    "https://app.netlas.io/api/host/23.215.0.136/" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $NETLAS_API_KEY"

# Domain query example
curl -s -X GET \
    "https://app.netlas.io/api/host/example.com/" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $NETLAS_API_KEY"

# Filtering output
curl -s -X GET \
"https://app.netlas.io/api/host/google.com/" \
  -G \
    --data-urlencode 'fields=whois.registrant.organization' \
    --data-urlencode 'source_type=include' \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $NETLAS_API_KEY"

# IP query example
netlas host "23.215.0.136" -f json

# Domain query example
netlas host "example.com"  -f json

# Filtering output
netlas host "google.com" \
  --format json \
  --include "whois.registrant.organization"

import netlas
import json

api_key = netlas.helpers.get_api_key()
netlas_connection = netlas.Netlas(api_key)

# IP query example
ip_info = netlas_connection.host(host="23.215.0.136")
print(json.dumps(ip_info))

# Domain query example
domain_info = netlas_connection.host(host="example.com")
print(json.dumps(domain_info))

# Filtering output
domain_info = netlas_connection.host(
  host="google.com", 
  fields="whois.registrant.organization"
)
print(json.dumps(domain_info))

get.description:
Retrieves the latest available data for the IP address of the client making the request.

Use this method to obtain information about your own IP address.

x-codeSamples.source:
import netlas
import json

api_key = netlas.helpers.get_api_key()
netlas_connection = netlas.Netlas(api_key)

ip_info = netlas_connection.host(host=None)
print(json.dumps(ip_info))

get.description:
Searches for `q` in the selected `indices` and returns up to 20 search results, starting from the `start+1` document.
  
❗️ This method allows retrieving only the first 10,000 search results.

Use it when the expected number of results is relatively low or to craft and refine a query.
Use the __Download__ endpoint to fetch results without pagination or quantity limitations.

ipRangeSearchExample.description: responses for a specific IP range

parameters.name: indices
parameters.description:
A list of [indices](#tag/Indices) where the search will be performed.

ℹ️ Unlike other search tools, Responses search does not have a default index.  
If `indices` is not specified, the search is conducted using the latest publicly available internet scan data, 
which is updated in real time.

DefaultIndiceSearch.description: Search in the latest data available

x-codeSamples.source:
import netlas
import json

query = "host:example.com"
fields = "ip,uri"

api_key = netlas.helpers.get_api_key()
netlas_connection = netlas.Netlas(api_key)

search_results = netlas_connection.search(query, fields=fields)
print(json.dumps(search_results))

get.description:
Counts the number of documents matching the search query `q` in the selected `indices`.

- If there are fewer than 1,000 results, the method returns the exact count.
- If there are more than 1,000 results, the count is estimated with an error margin not exceeding 3%.

parameters.name: indices
parameters.description:
A list of [indices](#tag/Indices) where the search will be performed.

ℹ️ Unlike other search tools, Responses search does not have a default index.  
If `indices` is not specified, the search is conducted using the latest publicly available internet scan data, 
which is updated in real time.

DefaultIndiceSearch.description: Search in the latest data available

x-codeSamples.source:
import netlas
import json

query = "ip:\"23.192.0.0/11\""

api_key = netlas.helpers.get_api_key()
netlas_connection = netlas.Netlas(api_key)

count_results = netlas_connection.count(query)
print(json.dumps(count_results))

post.description:
Retrieves `size` search results matching the query `q` in the selected `indices`.

The Netlas SDK and CLI tool additionally include a `download_all()` method and an `--all` key that allow you to query all available results.

x-codeSamples.source:
netlas download "ip:\"23.192.0.0/11\"" \
    --include domain,host,host_type,ip,path,port,prot4,prot7,protocol,ptr \
    --count 100 # --all to download all results (no need to sprcify --count)

import netlas
import json

query = "ip:\"23.192.0.0/11\""
fields = "domain,host,host_type,ip,path,port,prot4,prot7,protocol,ptr"

api_key = netlas.helpers.get_api_key()
netlas_connection = netlas.Netlas(api_key)
    
for r in netlas_connection.download(
    query, fields, size=100
    # use Netlas.download_all() to download all results
    # (no need to sprcify size parameter)
    ):
    response = json.loads(r.decode("utf-8"))
    print(json.dumps(response))

get.description: Searches for `q` in the selected `indices` and groups results by the `facets` field.

parameters.name: indices
parameters.description:
A list of [indices](#tag/Indices) where the search will be performed.

ℹ️ Unlike other search tools, Responses search does not have a default index.  
If `indices` is not specified, the search is conducted using the latest publicly available internet scan data, 
which is updated in real time.

DefaultIndiceSearch.description: Search in the latest data available

x-codeSamples.source:
import netlas
import json

query = "ip:\"23.192.0.0/11\""

api_key = netlas.helpers.get_api_key()
netlas_connection = netlas.Netlas(api_key)

results = netlas_connection.stat(
    query, group_fields="protocol"
)
print(json.dumps(results))

get.description:
Searches for `q` in the selected `indices` and returns up to 20 search results, starting from the `start+1` document.
  
❗️ This method allows retrieving only the first 10,000 search results.

Use it when the expected number of results is relatively low or to craft and refine a query.
Use the __Download__ endpoint to fetch results without pagination or quantity limitations.

domainSearchExample.description: subdomains of example.com with any A record

x-codeSamples.source:
import netlas
import json

query = "domain:example.com"
fields = "a,aaaa,ns,mx,txt"

api_key = netlas.helpers.get_api_key()
netlas_connection = netlas.Netlas(api_key)

search_results = netlas_connection.search(
    query, datatype="domain", fields=fields
    )
print(json.dumps(search_results))

get.description:
Counts the number of documents matching the search query `q` in the selected `indices`.

- If there are fewer than 1,000 results, the method returns the exact count.
- If there are more than 1,000 results, the count is estimated with an error margin not exceeding 3%.

x-codeSamples.source:
import netlas
import json

query = "domain:*.example.com a:*"

api_key = netlas.helpers.get_api_key()
netlas_connection = netlas.Netlas(api_key)

count_results = netlas_connection.count(
    query, datatype="domain"
    )
print(json.dumps(count_results))

post.description:
Retrieves `size` search results matching the query `q` in the selected `indices`.

The Netlas SDK and CLI tool additionally include a `download_all()` method and an `--all` key that allow you to query all available results.

x-codeSamples.source:
netlas download "domain:*.example.com a:*" \
    --datatype domain \
    --include domain,a \
    --count 100 # --all to download all results (no need to sprcify --count)

import netlas
import json

query = "domain:*.example.com a:*"
fields = "domain,a"

api_key = netlas.helpers.get_api_key()
netlas_connection = netlas.Netlas(api_key)
    
for r in netlas_connection.download(
    query, fields, datatype="domain" ,size=100
    # use Netlas.download_all() to download all results
    # (no need to sprcify size parameter)
    ):
    response = json.loads(r.decode("utf-8"))
    print(json.dumps(response))

netlas download "net.country:VA" \
    --datatype whois-ip \
    --include abuse,asn,net \
    --count 100 # --all to download all results (no need to sprcify --count)

import netlas
import json

query = "net.country:VA"
fields = "abuse,asn,net"

api_key = netlas.helpers.get_api_key()
netlas_connection = netlas.Netlas(api_key)
    
for r in netlas_connection.download(
    query, fields, datatype="whois-ip" ,size=100
    # use Netlas.download_all() to download all results
    # (no need to sprcify size parameter)
    ):
    response = json.loads(r.decode("utf-8"))
    print(json.dumps(response))

x-codeSamples.source:
netlas download "registrant.organization:\"Meta Platforms\"" \
    --datatype whois-domain \
    --include domain,registrant,registrar,created_date,updated_date \
    --count 100 # --all to download all results (no need to sprcify --count)

import netlas
import json

query = "registrant.organization:\"Meta Platforms\""
fields = "domain,registrant,registrar,created_date,updated_date"

api_key = netlas.helpers.get_api_key()
netlas_connection = netlas.Netlas(api_key)
    
for r in netlas_connection.download(
    query, fields, datatype="whois-domain" ,size=100
    # use Netlas.download_all() to download all results
    # (no need to sprcify size parameter)
    ):
    response = json.loads(r.decode("utf-8"))
    print(json.dumps(response))

get.description: Searches for `q` in the selected `indices` and groups results by the `facets` field.

Searches for `q` in the selected `indices` and returns up to 20 search results, starting from the `start+1` document.
  
❗️ This method allows retrieving only the first 10,000 search results.

Use it when the expected number of results is relatively low or to craft and refine a query.
Use the __Download__ endpoint to fetch results without pagination or quantity limitations.

parameters.name: indices
parameters.description:
ℹ️ Netlas stores all collected certificates in a single default index.  
This parameter is included for interface consistency but is not applicable to certificate-related endpoints.

x-codeSamples.source:
import netlas
import json

query = "certificate.subject_dn:example.com"

api_key = netlas.helpers.get_api_key()
netlas_connection = netlas.Netlas(api_key)

search_results = netlas_connection.search(
    query, datatype="cert"
    )
print(json.dumps(search_results))

Retrieves `size` search results matching the query `q` in the selected `indices`.

The Netlas SDK and CLI tool additionally include a `download_all()` method and an `--all` key that allow you to query all available results.

x-codeSamples.source:
netlas download "certificate.subject_dn:\"example.com\"" \
    --datatype cert \
    --include certificate.src,certificate.fingerprint_sha1 \
    --count 100 # --all to download all results (no need to sprcify --count)

import netlas
import json

query = "certificate.subject_dn:\"example.com\""
fields = "certificate.src,certificate.fingerprint_sha1"

api_key = netlas.helpers.get_api_key()
netlas_connection = netlas.Netlas(api_key)
    
for r in netlas_connection.download(
    query, fields, datatype="cert" ,size=100
    # use Netlas.download_all() to download all results
    # (no need to sprcify size parameter)
    ):
    response = json.loads(r.decode("utf-8"))
    print(json.dumps(response))

get.description: Retrieve a list of indices accessible to the user.

x-codeSamples.source:
import netlas
import json

api_key = netlas.helpers.get_api_key()
netlas_connection = netlas.Netlas(api_key)

indices = netlas_connection.indices()
print(json.dumps(indices))

parameters.name: collection
parameters.description: The collection for which mapping should be retrieved.

get.description:
Returns the facet-specific field mapping for the `{collection}`.

This mapping includes only fields that are suitable for use in **facet (group-by)** searches. 
Not all fields in the regular mapping can be used for aggregation, so fields that are not aggregatable 
(e.g., large text fields, arrays) are excluded from the facet mapping.

Use this endpoint to discover which fields are available for grouping results in your search queries.

parameters.name: collection
parameters.description: The collection for which facet mapping should be retrieved.

post.description: Retrieve a list of available searches for the specified node, defined by `node_value` and `node_type`.

schema.title: Available Searches
schema.description:
A list of aggregations containing available searches, the count of results for each search, 
and a preview of the results.

post.description:
Execute a search using `search_field_id` for a node specified by `node_value` and `node_type`, 
and retrieve the corresponding results.

parameters.name: X-Count-Id
parameters.description: Perform a [`/api/discovery/node_count`](#tag/Discovery/operation/getSearchesByNode) request to get the `X-Count-Id`.

aggregations.description: Search results returned by related to the performed search aggregation.

x-codeSamples.source:
if [ -z "$1" ]; then
    echo "Error: Pass X-Count-Id as an argument"
    exit 1
fi

curl -s -X POST \
    "https://app.netlas.io/api/discovery/node_result/" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $NETLAS_API_KEY" \
    -H "X-Count-Id: $1" \
    -d '{
        "node_type": "domain",
        "node_value": "example.com",
        "search_field_id": 29
    }'

post.description:
Retrieve a list of available searches for a group of nodes, 
where each node is specified in `node_value` and must be of type `node_type`.

⚠️ Retrieving results for large groups may take significant time.

schema.title: Stream of Available Searches

post.description:
Execute a search using `search_field_id` for a group of nodes specified in `node_value`, 
and retrieve the corresponding results.

⚠️ Retrieving results for large groups may take significant time.

parameters.name: X-Count-Id
parameters.description: Perform a [`/api/discovery/group_of_nodes_count`](#tag/Discovery/operation/getSearchesByGroup) request to get the `X-Count-Id`.

schema.title: Stream of Search Results

x-codeSamples.source:
if [ -z "$1" ]; then
    echo "Error: Pass X-Count-Id as an argument"
    exit 1
fi

curl -s -X POST \
    "https://app.netlas.io/api/discovery/group_of_nodes_result/" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $NETLAS_API_KEY" \
    -H "X-Count-Id: $1" \
    -d '{
        "node_type": "domain",
        "node_value": ["example.com", "example.org"],
        "search_field_id": 29
    }'

get.description:
Retrieve the current status of an ongoing group search operation:
[fetching available searches for a group](#tag/Discovery/operation/getSearchesByGroup) 
or [executing a search for a group](#tag/Discovery/operation/searchByGroup).
Retrieve a list of private scans available to the user.

parameters.name: own_scans_only
parameters.description: Filter to return only the scans owned by the user

post.description: Create and initiate a new private scan.

Modify the priority of a private scan with `id` in the processing queue by shifting it `shift` positions.

ℹ️ This adjustment applies only to scans with the `Pending` status. 
Scans that are already scheduled or in progress will not be affected.

x-codeSamples.source:
if [ -z "$1" ]; then
    echo "Error: Pass scan ID as an argument"
    exit 1
fi

curl -X 'POST' \
    "https://app.netlas.io/api/scanner/change_priority/" \
    -H "content-type: application/json" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $NETLAS_API_KEY" \
    -d '{"id": '"$1"', "shift":'-1'}'

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a private scan using its `id`.

x-codeSamples.source:
if [ -z "$1" ]; then
    echo "Error: Pass scan ID as an argument"
    exit 1
fi

curl -X GET "https://app.netlas.io/api/scanner/$1/" \
    -H "content-type: application/json" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $NETLAS_API_KEY"

patch.description: Update the label of a private scan using its `id`.

requestBody.description: The new label for the scan

x-codeSamples.source:
if [ -z "$1" ]; then
    echo "Error: Pass scan ID as an argument"
    exit 1
fi

curl -X 'PATCH' "https://app.netlas.io/api/scanner/$1/" \
    -H "content-type: application/json" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $NETLAS_API_KEY" \
    -d '{"label": "New Scan Label"}'

delete.description: Permanently remove a private scan using its `id`.

x-codeSamples.source:
if [ -z "$1" ]; then
    echo "Error: Pass scan ID as an argument"
    exit 1
fi

curl -X 'DELETE' "https://app.netlas.io/api/scanner/$1/" \
    -H "content-type: application/json" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $NETLAS_API_KEY"

post.description: Delete multiple private scans using their respective `id` values.

requestBody.description: The list of scan IDs to delete

ids.description: List of scan IDs to delete

x-codeSamples.source:
if [ -z "$1" ] || [ -z "$2" ]; then
    echo "Error: Pass two scan ID as arguments"
    exit 1
fi

curl -X POST "https://app.netlas.io/api/scanner/bulk_delete/" \
    -H "content-type: application/json" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $NETLAS_API_KEY" \
    -d '{ "ids": ['"$1"', '"$2"'] }'

patch.description: Modify the profile details of the currently authenticated user.

get.description: Retrieve the currently authenticated user's profile counters, including request limits and Netlas Coins balance.

BearerAuth.description:
Include your API key as a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header of each request.
Example:
```
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
```

field.description: Name of the field in the document.

fields.description:
You can control the amount of output data by specifying which fields to include or exclude in the response.
Use the `source_type` parameter to select between `include` and `exclude` options.

source_type.description:
Specify `fields` and use `source_type` to choose between:
- `include` to return only the specified fields
- `exclude` to return all fields except the specified ones

public_indices_only.description:
By default, data is requested from both [private and public indices](#tag/Indices), returning the most recent results.
Use this query parameter to restrict the search to public indices only.

ptr.title: PTR
ptr.description: A list of reverse DNS (PTR) records associated with the IP address.

geo.title: Geolocation
geo.description: Estimated geolocation and timezone information for the host.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.description: Indicates if this IP hosts a VPN, proxy, or Tor exit node.

organization.title: Organization
organization.description: The organization associated with the host.

IpWhoisDocument.description: WHOIS information for an IP address

example.raw:
Raw WHOIS data in the form of a string will be here.

The rest of the document contains the same data in parsed form.

ports.title: Ports
ports.description: A list of open ports detected during the scan.

software.title: Software
software.description: A list of software and technologies identified on the host.

tag.name: azure_cdn
tag.description: Azure Content Delivery Network (CDN) reduces load times, save bandwidth and speed responsiveness.

ioc.title: Indicators of Compromise
ioc.description: Indicators of compromise (IoCs) provided by third-party sources.

source.title: Source
source.description: A list of data sources where the information was retrieved.

domains.description: The first 10 domains associated with the IP address via DNS records.

domains_count.description: Total number of domains associated with the IP address

DomainsDocument.description: An object representing a set of DNS records for a domain

example.name: example
example.raw:
Raw WHOIS data in the form of a string will be here.

The rest of the document contains the same data in parsed form.

related_domains.description: The first 10 related domains associated with this domain.

related_domains_query.description: Query to find related domains in [DNS Search](#tag/Domains).

enum: You can upgrade your plan to get more Netlas Coins or wait until your subscription renews.

detail.description: Request to wait a number of seconds before retrying the request.

q.description: The query string used for searching. See [Query Syntax](/knowledge-base/query-language/) for more details.

start.description: Use for pagination. Offset from the first search result to return.

indiceLabel.description: Unique text identifier for the index.

indices.description:
A list of indice labels to search in.
If not provided, the search will be performed in the default (most relevant index).

Call [indices](#tag/Indices) endpoint to get the list of available indices.

highlight.description: A pattern matched in the document, used for highlighting data in the search results

certificate.issuer_dn: C=US, O=DigiCert Inc, CN=DigiCert Global G3 TLS ECC SHA384 2020 CA1

chain.issuer_dn: C=US, O=DigiCert Inc, OU=www.digicert.com, CN=DigiCert Global Root G3

common_name: DigiCert Global G3 TLS ECC SHA384 2020 CA1

ResponsesDownloadCSV.description: A CSV file with search results.

enum: You have to be signed in to perform this operation.

facets.description: A list of fields to group search results by.

facet_search_size.description: Number of search results to return for each facet.

aggregations.description: A list of aggregated search results.

doc_count.description: A number of document with the given key in the search results

items.description: 0 to 20 documents that match the search query

scan_started_at.description: Timestamp indicating when data collection started.

scan_ended_at.description: Timestamp indicating when data collection was completed.

is_default.description: Indicates if this is the default index for the data collection.

speed.description: Access speed of the index. Older indices are moved to slower storage.

state.description: Current state of the index. `indexing` indicates that data is still being collected.

count.description: Count of items in the index.

category.description: Top level fields are grouped to categories for better organization.

field_type.description: Field type determine how different parts of documents are stored and queried.

children.description: The nested fields under the parent field.

ip.description: The IP address that was requested to fetch the response.

domain.description: A list of domains associated with the IP-address.

host.description:
The IP address or the domain name used in a request. 
This field is different from the `target` field for responses captured after redirects.

host_type.description: The type of the host field. Could be `domain` or `ip`.

path.description:
The path that was requested.
In the most cases it equals to "/", but can be different in case of 301 or 302 redirects.

port.description: The port number that was used to connect to the service.

prot4.description:
The protocol used on the transport layer.
Currently Netlas uses `tcp` and `udp` protocols.

prot7.description:
The application-layer protocol, excluding secure variants.

Common values include `http`, `ftp`, `smtp`, etc.  
Secure versions like `https` or `ftps` are **not** included in this field.

For example, the query `prot7:t3` is equivalent to `protocol:(t3 OR t3s)`.

protocol.description:
The protocol used in the request, including both standard and secure variants.

Common values include `http`, `https`, `ftp`, `ftps`, `smtp`, etc.  
This field includes secure versions where applicable.

The query `protocol:(http OR https)` returns the same results as `prot7:http`.

ptr.description: The pointer record (PTR) associated with the IP address, if available.

referer.description: A source of redirect in responses captured after redirects.

target.description:
The initial target of scan, IP or domain. 
This field contains subfileds `target.type`, `target.ip`, and `target.domain`.

uri.description: The full URI that was scanned, including the scheme, host, path, and query parameters.

certificate.description:
The X.509 certificate fetched during the scan. 
This field contains nested fields for various attributes of the certificate.

cve.description: Contains CVE-specific data, including vulnerability details and references.

geo.description: Contains geographical information related to the IP, such as country and city.

jarm.description: Contains JARM fingerprint data for identifying TLS servers.

isp.description: Contains information about the Internet Service Provider (ISP) associated with the IP.

tag.description: Contains technology and software tags assigned to the response.

whois.description: Contains IP WHOIS data for the scanned IP address.

amqp.description: Contains parsed data specific to the AMQP protocol.

dns.description: Contains parsed data specific to DNS communication.

elasticsearch.description: Contains parsed data from Elasticsearch protocol responses.

ftp.description: Contains protocol-specific fields extracted from FTP banners or responses.

http.description: Contains HTTP-specific data such as headers, status codes, and response body fragments.

imap.description: Contains parsed data specific to the IMAP protocol.

memcached.description: Contains protocol-specific data extracted from Memcached services.

modbus.description: Contains Modbus-specific data, including function codes and device responses.

mongodb.description: Contains MongoDB-specific metadata and handshake information.

mqtt.description: Contains MQTT protocol details such as connection flags and broker info.

mssql.description: Contains parsed metadata from Microsoft SQL Server protocol responses.

mysql.description: Contains MySQL-specific handshake and version information.

netbios.description: Contains data extracted from NetBIOS responses, such as name services.

ntp.description: Contains NTP-specific data including version and time sync fields.

oracle.description: Contains parsed metadata from Oracle database protocol responses.

pop3.description: Contains POP3-specific information from banner or server greeting.

postgres.description: Contains metadata and handshake data from PostgreSQL services.

raw_tcp.description: Contains raw TCP data for protocols that are not explicitly parsed.

rdp.description: Contains metadata extracted from Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) responses.

redis.description: Contains Redis-specific banner and configuration data.

s7.description: Contains data from Siemens S7 industrial control systems.

smb.description: Contains parsed information from SMB protocol responses.

smtp.description: Contains SMTP-specific data such as greeting banners and capabilities.

snmp.description: Contains SNMP response data including system info and MIBs.

socks.description: Contains metadata extracted from SOCKS proxy services.

ssh.description: Contains SSH protocol metadata such as version and key exchange details.

t3.description: Contains data extracted from the T3 protocol (used by Oracle WebLogic).

telnet.description: Contains data extracted from Telnet service banners or negotiation.

vnc.description: Contains VNC-specific data such as protocol version and authentication methods.

scan_date.description: The date when the scan was performed.

@timestamp.description: The timestamp when the document was indexed.

last_updated.description: The timestamp when the document was last updated.

domain.description: The domain name associated with the record.

a.description: The IPv4 address records for the domain.

aaaa.description: The IPv6 address records for the domain.

cname.description: The Canonical Name (CNAME) records for the domain.

mx.description: The Mail Exchange (MX) records for the domain.

ns.description: The Name Server (NS) records for the domain.

txt.description: The Text (TXT) records for the domain, often used for verification or metadata.

zone.description: The zone information for the domain, defining its DNS boundaries.

level.description: The hierarchical level of the domain in the DNS structure.

@timestamp.description: The timestamp when the document was indexed.

last_updated.description: The timestamp when the document was last updated.

ip.description: The IP range associated with the WHOIS record.

abuse.description: Information about abuse contacts related to the IP.

asn.description:
The autonomous system details for the IP, including the ASN number, registry, 
country, name, CIDR range, and the last updated date.

net.description:
The network block or range associated with the IP, including details such as 
the country, address, city, organization, CIDR range, and contact information.

related_nets.description:
Other network blocks or ranges related to the IP.
The structure is similar to the `net` field.

raw.description: The raw WHOIS data in form of unparsed text.

@timestamp.description: The timestamp when the document was indexed.

id.description: The identifier assigned by the registrar for the domain.

domain.description: The domain for which the data was requested.

name.description: The name of the domain (without zone).

zone.description: The zone information for the domain.

level.description: The hierarchical level of the domain in the DNS structure.

punycode.description: The punycode representation of the domain.

extracted_domain.description:
The domain name, extracted from the received WHOIS server response.
Could countains national characters, e.g. "看剧.vin".

extension.description: The zone of the domain, extracted from the received WHOIS server response.

created_date.description: The date when the domain was created.

updated_date.description: The date when the domain was last updated.

expiration_date.description: The expiration date of the domain.

status.description:
Represents the current status flags assigned to the domain name as defined by ICANN and registrars.
These flags indicate restrictions or permissions on domain operations such as transfers, deletions, or updates.

Common values include:
- `clientTransferProhibited`: The domain cannot be transferred without registrar intervention.
- `clientDeleteProhibited`: Prevents the domain from being deleted.
- `clientUpdateProhibited`: Prevents updates to domain information.
- `serverHold`: Domain is not in the DNS and will not resolve.
- `ok`: No restrictions — the domain is in good standing.

registrant.description: Registrant information for the domain, including details such as organization, address, and contact.

registrar.description: Registrar information for the domain, including details such as organization, address, and contact.

technical.description: Technical contact information for the domain.

billing.description: Billing contact information for the domain.

administrative.description: Administrative contact information for the domain.

server.description: The server that was used to request the WHOIS data.

whois_server.description: The WHOIS server specified in the response as authoritative for the domain.

name_servers.description: The name servers associated with the domain.

dnssec.description: Indicates whether DNSSEC is enabled for the domain.

raw.description: The raw WHOIS data in form of unparsed text.

@timestamp.description: The timestamp when the document was indexed.

last_updated.description: The timestamp when the document was last updated.

certificate.description:
The certificate information, including the subject and issuer details.
This field contains nested fields for various attributes of the certificate.

@timestamp.description: The timestamp when the document was indexed.

last_updated.description: The timestamp when the document was last updated.

X-Count-Id.description: The Discovery tool tracks sets of available searches associated with a node or group using this ID.

search_field.title: Search Field
search_field.description: The type of search that describes how the search results relate to this object.

count.title: Count
count.description: The number of results for this search type.

preview.title: Preview
preview.description: Up to five found items as a preview.

search_field_id.title: Search Field ID
search_field_id.description: The ID of the search type.

aggregation.title: Aggregation
aggregation.description: An aggregation representing a search type for a node.

is_too_much_docs.description: Indicates that the search returns too many documents to add individually, and they should be added as a group.

aggregations.title: Aggregations
aggregations.description:
Aggregations are made for data collections. 
Each aggregation contains searches for related entities from this data collection.

is_valid.description: Indicates that the search result should be ignored due to an inappropriate format.

X-Stream-Id.description: The unique identifier of a stream used to [track the progress of a search](#tag/Discovery/operation/getGroupRequestStatus).

data.description: The search status object contains the following fields:

scan_label.description: A system-defined unique label for the index associated with the scan. Used as an identifier.

targets.description:
A list of targets for the scan.  
Valid targets include IP addresses, domain names, and CIDR ranges.

scan_started_dt.description: Timestamp indicating when the scan started.

scan_ended_dt.description: Timestamp indicating when the scan ended.

scan_progress.description: Provides real-time details about the scan’s progress.

id.description: Unique identifier for the scan stage.

state.description: The current state of the scan process.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of the scan progress update.

created_dt.description: Timestamp indicating when the scan was created.

saved_graph.description: If the scan is associated with a saved attack surface graph, this field contains the graph ID.

estimated_time_to_complete.description: Estimated time in seconds to complete the scan.

queue_position.description: Position of the scan in the processing queue.

count.description: Number of items in the index (e.g. number of collected responses).

owner.description: Information about the owner of the scan.

email.description: Email address of the scan owner.

first_name.description: First name of the scan owner.

last_name.description: Last name of the scan owner.

teams.description: List of teams the scan owner belongs to.

is_scan_from_team.description: Indicates whether the scan was initiated by another team member instead of the current user.

scan_progress.description: 8/8 - Collecting data from non-standard exposed ports

shift.description:
The number of positions to adjust the scan's priority in the queue.

- A _negative value_ (e.g., `-2`) moves the scan closer to the front, prioritizing it for _earlier execution_.
- A _positive value_ (e.g., `+3`) moves the scan further back, _delaying its execution_.

dataset_id.description: A unique identifier for the dataset.

product_id.description:
A unique identifier for the product.

Datasets belong to Product objects.
A product can include one or multiple datasets.

name.description: The internal system name of the dataset.

price.description: Price of the dataset in USD.

added_dt.description: Date and time when the dataset was published.

ext.description: Formats in which the dataset is available.

fields.description: List of fields in the dataset.

search_query.description:
The search query linked to this dataset.
Use this query to find similar data in Netlas Search tools (see `data_type`).

size.description: Size of the dataset in bytes.

data_type.description:
The type of data contained in the dataset:
- 1: Response data
- 2: Domain data
- 4: PTR records
- 6: IP WHOIS data
- 7: Domain WHOIS data

id.description: A unique identifier for the product.

datasets.description: List of datasets included in the product.

data_category.description: Object representing the category of the product.

count.description: Total count of items in the product.

size.description: Size of the product in bytes.

name.description: System symbolic name of the product.

fields.description: List of fields in the product.

price.description: Price of the product in USD.

meta_data.description: Additional metadata for the search engines.

example.description: Known domain names with A, MX,  NS, CNAME and TXT records.

datasets.name: whole_dns_registry
datasets.description: Known domain names with A, MX,  NS, CNAME and TXT records.

data_category.name: DNS Registry Data
data_category.description: Netlas.io collects domain names from many sources, including ICANN Centralized Zone Data Service, SSL Certificates, 301 & 302 HTTP redirects (during Internet scans) and others.

example.name: whole_dns_registry

keywords: DOMAIN NAME SYSTEM REGISTRY DATA > FORWARD DNS DATASET (ALL TYPE OF RECORDS)
meta_data.name: Forward DNS (fDNS)
meta_data.description: Known domain names with A, MX,  NS, CNAME and TXT records.

name.description: The internal system name of the dataset link.

links.description: List of links for the dataset.

pretty_name.description: Human-readable (pretty) name of the dataset link.

api_key.description: Information about the user's API key.

next_time_coins_will_be_updated.description: The next date when coins will be updated for this API key.

is_welcome_tour_passed.description: Indicates if the user has completed the welcome tour.

plan.description: Information about the user's subscription plan.

plan_active_until.description: The date until the current plan is active.

next_plan.description: Information about the subscription plan that will be activated after the current one expires.

is_next_plan_paid.description: Indicates if the next subscription period is paid (used for one-time purchases only).

next_plan_paid_period.description: The paid period for the next plan.

bonuses.description: The number of bonus points available to the user.

notifications.description: A list of subscriptions and notification channels.

teams.description: A list of teams the user is part of.

is_recurring.description: Indicates if the subscription is recurring.

is_authenticated_via_social.description: Indicates whether the user is authenticated via a third-party service.

referral_link_usage_amount.description: The number of times the referral link has been used.

user_counters.title: User Counters
user_counters.description: Usage statistics for the user, including API requests, Netlas Coins, and scan coins.

requests_left.description: Details about the user's remaining API request quota.

remained.description: The number of remaining requests. A value of `-1` indicates unlimited requests.

limit.description: The maximum number of requests allowed. A value of `-1` indicates no limit.

will_be_updated.description: The date when the request limit will be updated. Can be `null` if not applicable.

coins.description: Details about the user's Netlas Coins usage and allocation.

total_coins_spent.description: The total number of coins spent by the user.

left.description: The number of coins left for the user. A value of `-1` indicates unlimited coins.

plan_coins_amount.description: The total number of coins allocated in the user's plan. A value of `-1` indicates unlimited coins.

next_time_coins_will_be_updated.description: The next date when coins will be updated.

scan_coins.description: Details about the user's scan coins allocation and usage.

plan_scan_coins_amount.description: The total number of scan coins allocated in the user's plan. A value of `-1` indicates unlimited scan coins.

left.description: The number of scan coins left for the user. A value of `-1` indicates unlimited scan coins.

reserved_coins.description: The number of scan coins reserved for ongoing operations.

BAD_REQUEST.description: __Bad Request__: Some required parameters were not passed or were not validated

PAYMENT_REQUIRED.description: __Not Enough Coins__: You are out of your current subscription plan limits

TOO_MANY_REQUESTS.description:
__Daily request limit exceeded__ or __Request throttled__. 
Additional error details are provided in the response body

enum: You can upgrade your plan to increase the daily rate limit or wait until the limit resets.

Retry-After.description: Indicates the number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.description: __Internal Server Error__: Details are included in the response body

TIMEOUT.description: __Timeout__: The request took too long to process

enum: Unable to process the request at this time. Please try again later.

ACCESS_DENIED.description:
__Access Denied__: You are not allowed to perform this action
Error details are included in the response body.

UNAUTHORIZED.description: __Unauthorized__: The request was rejected due to missing or invalid authentication credentials

NOT_FOUND.description: __Not Found__: Requested resource could not be found

_errors_NOT_FOUND.description: __Not Found__: The scan with the requested ID was not found

example.detail: The scan with ID 1234 was not found in the user's scans.

_errors_PAYMENT_REQUIRED.description: __Payment Required__: This product requires payment on your subscription plan

enum: This product requires payment on your subscription plan.

_errors_BAD_REQUEST.description: __Bad Request__: The API key is missing, invalid, or incorrectly formatted

_errors_ACCESS_DENIED.description: __Access Denied__: Changing this field is not allowed

example.title: Access Denied
example.detail: Changing this field is not allowed.

tags.name: Host Info
tags.description: Retrieves a summary of Netlas data for a specific IP address or domain name. The Host Info endpoint supports queries by IP or domain only, but returns aggregated data from all Netlas data collections in a single response. The same data available in the Netlas web interface via the IP/Domain Info tool.

tags.name: Responses
tags.description: Access continuously updated internet scan data collected by Netlas. The same data available in the Netlas web interface via the Responses Search tool.

tags.name: Domains
tags.description: Information about domain names, their corresponding IP addresses, and other types of DNS records. The same data available in the Netlas web interface via the DNS Search tool.

tags.name: IP WHOIS
tags.description: Provides information on IP address ownership, network provider details, contact information, and other WHOIS data. The same data available in the Netlas web interface via the IP WHOIS Search tool.

tags.name: Domain WHOIS
tags.description: Information on domain ownership, registrar details, contact information, registration dates, expiration dates, and other WHOIS data. The same data available in the Netlas web interface via the Domain WHOIS Search tool.

tags.name: Certificates
tags.description: A collection of x.509 certificates gathered from various sources. The same data available in the Netlas web interface via the Certificates Search tool.

tags.name: Indices
tags.description:
Netlas organizes its data into indices. Each search tool has a dedicated set of indices with data collected on specific dates.

Responses search tool also provides access to private indices:
- __Public indices__: regular Netlas indices available to all Netlas users.
- __Private indices__: indices produced by using Private Scanner by you or shared with you by your teammates.

tags.name: Mapping
tags.description:
Netlas stores collected data as JSON documents, organized into several data collections, such as Responses and Domains.
Each data collection has its own mapping, which defines its document structure.  

This group of methods provides access to the mapping of each data collection.

tags.name: Discovery
tags.description: The Attack Surface Discovery tool helps explore and analyze relationships between internet entities such as IP addresses, domain names, networks, WHOIS records, and more. This group of endpoints is used by the Discovery tool in the Netlas web app.

tags.name: Scanner
tags.description: The Netlas Private Scanner performs non-intrusive scans of internet-connected assets. This group of endpoints is used by the Private Scanner tool in the Netlas web app.

tags.name: Datastore
tags.description: The Netlas Datastore API provides access to a wide range of network-related datasets. Use these endpoints to access the Netlas Datastore.

externalDocs.description: If you're new to the Netlas web app, we recommend starting here.
